intro.title: Reproducibility Concepts
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Reproducibility Concepts is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode: print("Same data + code + environment => same results")

challenges.title: Easy: Recognize Reproducibility Concepts
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code related to **Reproducibility Concepts**. Include: `print`.

challenges.title: Medium: Complete the pattern
challenges.promptMarkdown: Complete code that demonstrates **Reproducibility Concepts**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Same data + code + environment => same results")

challenges.title: Hard: Apply the concept
challenges.promptMarkdown: Apply **Reproducibility Concepts** in a short R snippet.

questions.prompt: Reproducible research means:

choices.label: Others can recreate your results from your materials
Results change each run by design

questions.prompt: Name one R keyword or function from this topic.
This topic is important in R because:
